resolution: Fixed - Added comprehensive cache-busting headers to both HTML responses and static file serving. Implemented Cache-Control: no-cache, Pragma: no-cache, Expires: 0 headers plus timestamp-based cache buster in HTML meta tags.
title: Admin UI Needs Forced Browser Cache Clearing on Reload
description: Admin UI needs to force clear browser cache on reload to ensure latest version is running. Users may be running stale cached versions of the UI after updates.

root_cause_potential: Lack of cache-busting mechanisms in frontend builds
Missing cache control headers for static assets
No versioning strategy for UI updates
Browser caching of HTML/JS/CSS files preventing updates
technical_analysis.suggested_solution: Implement cache-busting strategies including versioned asset URLs, proper cache control headers, and force reload mechanisms

immediate_fixes.action: Add cache-busting query parameters to static assets (CSS/JS)
immediate_fixes.rationale: Ensure browsers fetch latest versions of updated files

immediate_fixes.action: Implement proper HTTP cache control headers
immediate_fixes.rationale: Control browser caching behavior for different asset types

immediate_fixes.action: Add version stamps to HTML templates
immediate_fixes.rationale: Force browser to recognize when UI has been updated

immediate_fixes.action: Consider adding a 'force refresh' button in Admin UI
immediate_fixes.rationale: Provide users manual control over cache clearing

impact_assessment.user_impact: Medium - Users may see outdated UI and miss important updates
impact_assessment.functionality_impact: Medium - Stale UI may not work correctly with updated backend
impact_assessment.urgency: Medium - Should be addressed to ensure users get latest features and fixes
